title: Umami Flaky Lazy-Sunday Casabe Stir-fry
description: This pescatarian-friendly Umami Flaky Lazy-Sunday Casabe Stir-fry combines the savory umami flavors of pressure-cooked casabe with a variety of vegetables and spices, making it a perfect Sunday brunch dish.
instructions: Preheat your pressure cooker according to the manufacturer's instructions.
Slice the casabe into thin, flaky pieces. If using a fresh casabe that has not been baked yet, prepare it by soaking in water for about an hour and then slicing as directed.
Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in the preheated pressure cooker over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic cloves to the hot oil and sauté until fragrant (about 1 minute).
Add mixed vegetables, paprika, salt, and red pepper flakes if using. Stir-fry for about 5 minutes or until vegetables are just tender.

Gently fold in casabe slices, ensuring they do not break apart too much during the stir-frying process. Cook for an additional minute to allow casabe to slightly soften and absorb flavors from the stir-fry mixture.
Transfer the finished dish onto a serving platter and serve hot.
